text: This study aims to determine the Maduranese personal and social attitudes towards Carok and what factors caused Carok to still exist. This research used descriptive qualitative method. The material was obtained from 195 Maduranese people by means of direct observation, questionnaire and depth interview. The results show that the Maduranese people's personal attitude towards Carok had changed where 75% of respondents did not intend to carry out Carok, 60% of respondents never carried out Carok although the stimulus for Carok was provided, 77.38% of respondents preferred for the conflict to be wisely solved, and 77.40% of respondents carried out Islamic Sharia law and State law. Moreover, 64.16% of respondents stated that Carok was not part of Madurese tradition, and 80% of respondents stated that Carok was against Maduranese norms which caused Carok to be classified into inauspicious and not Madurese traditions, where 86.11% of respondents agreed that Carok was not Madurese culture and 82.44% of respondents stated that Carok was a harmful tradition. The old tradition factors that still made Carok exist were related to woman, misunderstanding, inheritance disputes, religious believes, theft and debt.
